title: Visual Proof Sketch of the Schröder-Bernstein Theorem
description: A diagram illustrating the core idea behind the proof of the Schröder-Bernstein theorem, which states that if there exist injective functions between two sets in both directions, then there exists a bijection between them. It shows how to partition a set to construct the required bijection.
elements: Partition of set A into subsets A0, A1, A2, ... (via the chain construction)
relationships: f and g are the given injections
The bijection h is defined as: h(a) = f(a) if a is in some A_n (n even), and h(a) = g⁻¹(a) if a is in some A_n (n odd)
